quiz.title: Macroeconomics (Quiz 4)
quiz.subtitle: Unit 5 Quiz 4: Tax Multiplier (20 Calculation Questions)
quiz.subject: Economics
quiz.topic: Fiscal Policy, Tax Multiplier, MPC, MPS

questions.question: If the MPC is 0.75, what is the value of the tax multiplier?
questions.explanation: Tax multiplier = -MPC / (1 - MPC) = -0.75 / 0.25 = -3.

questions.question: The MPC is 0.8. The tax multiplier is:
questions.explanation: Tax multiplier = -MPC / (1 - MPC) = -0.8 / 0.2 = -4.

questions.question: If the MPS is 0.25 and MPC is 0.75, the tax multiplier is:
questions.explanation: Tax multiplier = -MPC / MPS = -0.75 / 0.25 = -3.

questions.question: The MPC is 0.9. A \$50 billion tax cut will increase GDP by:
questions.explanation: Tax multiplier = -0.9 / 0.1 = -9. GDP change = -1.5 × Change in taxes? Wait: Tax multiplier = -9. A \$50B tax cut means ΔT = -\$50B. ΔGDP = -9 × (-\$50B) = +\$450 billion.

questions.question: If the MPC is 0.6, a \$100 billion tax increase will cause GDP to:
questions.explanation: Tax multiplier = -0.6 / 0.4 = -1.5. GDP change = -1.5 × \$100B = -\$150 billion.

questions.question: The MPS is 0.2. To increase GDP by \$400 billion through a tax cut, taxes must be cut by:
questions.explanation: MPC = 0.8. Tax multiplier = -0.8 / 0.2 = -4. Required tax cut = \$400B / 4 = \$100 billion.

questions.question: If the tax multiplier is -2.5, what is the MPC?
The MPC is 0.5. A \$200 billion tax increase will change GDP by:
questions.explanation: Tax multiplier = -0.5 / 0.5 = -1. GDP change = -1 × \$200B = -\$200 billion.

questions.question: If the MPS is 0.1, a \$20 billion tax cut will increase GDP by:
questions.explanation: MPC = 0.9. Tax multiplier = -0.9 / 0.1 = -9. GDP change = -9 × (-\$20B) = +\$180 billion.

questions.question: The tax multiplier is -4. To close a \$120 billion recessionary gap, taxes must be:
questions.explanation: Required tax cut = Gap / |Tax Multiplier| = \$120B / 4 = \$30 billion.

questions.question: The MPC is 0.75. A \$60 billion tax increase will cause GDP to:
questions.explanation: Tax multiplier = -0.75 / 0.25 = -3. GDP change = -3 × \$60B = -\$180 billion.

questions.question: If households consume 80% of additional income, the tax multiplier is:
questions.explanation: MPC = 0.8. Tax multiplier = -MPC / (1 - MPC) = -0.8 / 0.2 = -4.

questions.question: The tax multiplier is -1.5. If taxes are cut by \$80 billion, GDP will:
questions.explanation: A tax cut means ΔT = -\$80B. GDP change = Tax multiplier × ΔT = -1.5 × (-\$80B) = +\$120 billion.

questions.question: If the MPC falls from 0.8 to 0.6, the absolute value of the tax multiplier:
questions.explanation: MPC = 0.8 → |Tax multiplier| = |-4| = 4. MPC = 0.6 → |-1.5| = 1.5. The absolute value decreases.

questions.question: The MPS is 0.4. To reduce an inflationary gap of \$90 billion, taxes must be:
questions.explanation: MPC = 0.6. Tax multiplier = -0.6 / 0.4 = -1.5. Required tax increase = \$90B / 1.5 = \$60 billion.

questions.question: The MPC is 0.8. If the government cuts taxes by \$25 billion and increases spending by \$25 billion, the net effect on GDP is:
questions.explanation: Spending multiplier = 5 → +\$125B. Tax multiplier = -4 → tax cut adds \$100B. Net effect = \$125B + \$100B? Wait: ΔG = +\$25B × 5 = +\$125B. ΔT = -\$25B × -4 = +\$100B. Net = +\$25B. This illustrates the balanced budget multiplier of 1.

questions.question: If the tax multiplier is -3, a \$40 billion tax cut will increase consumption spending initially by:
questions.explanation: Tax multiplier = -MPC/(1-MPC) = -3, so MPC = 0.75. Initial consumption increase = Tax cut × MPC = \$40B × 0.75 = \$30 billion.

questions.question: The MPC is 0.9. To decrease GDP by \$270 billion to fight inflation, taxes must be:
questions.explanation: Tax multiplier = -0.9 / 0.1 = -9. Required tax increase = \$270B / 9 = \$30 billion.

questions.question: If the tax multiplier is -2, what is the MPS?
questions.explanation: -MPC / MPS = -2. MPC / MPS = 2. MPC = 2MPS. Since MPC + MPS = 1: 2MPS + MPS = 1. 3MPS = 1. MPS ≈ 0.333.

questions.question: The MPS is 0.2. A \$50 billion tax increase combined with a \$50 billion spending increase will:
questions.explanation: Spending multiplier = 1 / 0.2 = 5 → +\$250B. Tax multiplier = -0.8 / 0.2 = -4 → -\$200B. Net effect = \$250B - \$200B = +\$50 billion.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You have mastered the tax multiplier and can apply it accurately in fiscal policy scenarios.
scoreMessages.70: Good work! You understand the core formula. Remember: Tax multiplier = -MPC/(1-MPC). It is always smaller in absolute value than the spending multiplier.
scoreMessages.50: A fair attempt. Focus on: 1) Tax multiplier is negative. 2) |Tax Multiplier| = MPC / MPS. 3) A tax cut increases GDP; a tax hike decreases it.
scoreMessages.0: Keep practicing! Remember: The tax multiplier is weaker than the spending multiplier because the first round of a tax cut is partly saved (MPS), not fully spent.
